examples.title: Pseudocode: Graph ADT Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates the core operations of the Graph Abstract Data Type including create, insert vertex/edge, remove, and query operations
examples.code:
ALGORITHM GraphCreate
INPUT: maxVertices
OUTPUT: Empty graph

BEGIN
 SET graph.vertices = empty list
 SET graph.edges = empty list 
 SET graph.adjList = empty map
 RETURN graph
END

ALGORITHM InsertVertex
INPUT: graph G, vertex v
OUTPUT: Updated graph G

BEGIN
 IF v NOT IN G.vertices THEN
 ADD v TO G.vertices
 SET G.adjList[v] = empty list
 END IF
 RETURN G
END

ALGORITHM InsertEdge
INPUT: graph G, vertex u, vertex v, isDirected, weight (optional)
OUTPUT: Updated graph G

BEGIN
 IF u NOT IN G.vertices THEN
 CALL InsertVertex(G, u)
 END IF
 IF v NOT IN G.vertices THEN
 CALL InsertVertex(G, v)
 END IF
 
 IF weight IS DEFINED THEN
 ADD (v, weight) TO G.adjList[u]
 IF NOT isDirected THEN
 ADD (u, weight) TO G.adjList[v]
 END IF
 ELSE
 ADD v TO G.adjList[u]
 IF NOT isDirected THEN
 ADD u TO G.adjList[v]
 END IF
 END IF
 
 ADD (u, v) TO G.edges
 RETURN G
END

ALGORITHM RemoveEdge
INPUT: graph G, vertex u, vertex v
OUTPUT: Updated graph G

BEGIN
 REMOVE v FROM G.adjList[u]
 REMOVE u FROM G.adjList[v]
 REMOVE (u, v) FROM G.edges
 RETURN G
END

ALGORITHM RemoveVertex
INPUT: graph G, vertex v
OUTPUT: Updated graph G

BEGIN
 FOR EACH vertex u IN G.vertices DO
 REMOVE v FROM G.adjList[u]
 END FOR
 REMOVE v FROM G.adjList
 REMOVE v FROM G.vertices
 RETURN G
END

ALGORITHM IsAdjacent
INPUT: graph G, vertex u, vertex v
OUTPUT: Boolean

BEGIN
 IF v IN G.adjList[u] THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
END

ALGORITHM GetNeighbors
INPUT: graph G, vertex v
OUTPUT: List of adjacent vertices

BEGIN
 IF v IN G.adjList THEN
 RETURN G.adjList[v]
 ELSE
 RETURN empty list
 END IF
END

ALGORITHM GetDegree
INPUT: graph G, vertex v, isDirected
OUTPUT: Degree of vertex v

BEGIN
 IF isDirected THEN
 outDeg = SIZE OF G.adjList[v]
 inDeg = 0
 FOR EACH vertex u IN G.vertices DO
 IF v IN G.adjList[u] THEN
 inDeg = inDeg + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN (inDeg, outDeg)
 ELSE
 RETURN SIZE OF G.adjList[v]
 END IF
END

ALGORITHM IsConnected
INPUT: graph G
OUTPUT: Boolean

BEGIN
 IF G.vertices IS EMPTY THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 END IF
 
 SET visited = empty set
 SET queue = empty queue
 
 startVertex = FIRST element of G.vertices
 ADD startVertex TO queue
 ADD startVertex TO visited
 
 WHILE queue IS NOT EMPTY DO
 current = REMOVE FROM queue
 FOR EACH neighbor IN G.adjList[current] DO
 IF neighbor NOT IN visited THEN
 ADD neighbor TO visited
 ADD neighbor TO queue
 END IF
 END FOR
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN (SIZE OF visited == SIZE OF G.vertices)
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the core Graph ADT operations. The operations include: (1) Create - initializes an empty graph, (2) InsertVertex - adds a new vertex, (3) InsertEdge - connects two vertices (supports directed/undirected and weighted edges), (4) RemoveEdge - deletes an edge, (5) RemoveVertex - deletes a vertex and all incident edges, (6) IsAdjacent - checks if two vertices are directly connected, (7) GetNeighbors - returns all adjacent vertices, (8) GetDegree - calculates in-degree and out-degree for directed graphs, and (9) IsConnected - uses BFS to check if all vertices are reachable from any starting vertex.

examples.title: Python: Graph Implementation with Adjacency List
examples.description: Complete Python implementation of Graph ADT using adjacency list representation with support for directed, undirected, and weighted graphs
examples.code:
"""
Graph Abstract Data Type Implementation
Using Adjacency List Representation
Supports: Directed, Undirected, Weighted Graphs
"""

class Graph:
 def __init__(self, directed=False, weighted=False):
 """
 Initialize an empty graph
 
 Args:
 directed: If True, creates a directed graph
 weighted: If True, edges will have weights
 """
 self.vertices = set()
 self.adj_list = {} # Adjacency list: vertex -> list of (neighbor, weight)
 self.directed = directed
 self.weighted = weighted
 self.edges = [] # List of (u, v, weight) tuples
 
 def add_vertex(self, v):
 """Insert a vertex into the graph"""
 if v not in self.vertices:
 self.vertices.add(v)
 self.adj_list[v] = []
 return self
 
 def add_edge(self, u, v, weight=None):
 """
 Insert an edge between vertices u and v
 
 Args:
 u: Source vertex
 v: Destination vertex
 weight: Weight of the edge (if weighted graph)
 """
 # Add vertices if they don't exist
 self.add_vertex(u)
 self.add_vertex(v)
 
 # Handle weighted edges
 if self.weighted:
 w = weight if weight is not None else 1
 self.adj_list[u].append((v, w))
 if not self.directed:
 self.adj_list[v].append((u, w))
 self.edges.append((v, u, w))
 self.edges.append((u, v, w))
 else:
 # Unweighted edge
 self.adj_list[u].append(v)
 if not self.directed:
 self.adj_list[v].append(u)
 self.edges.append((v, u, 1))
 self.edges.append((u, v, 1))
 
 return self
 
 def remove_edge(self, u, v):
 """Remove the edge between u and v"""
 if u not in self.adj_list or v not in self.adj_list:
 return self
 
 if self.weighted:
 self.adj_list[u] = [(neighbor, w) for neighbor, w in self.adj_list[u] if neighbor != v]
 if not self.directed:
 self.adj_list[v] = [(neighbor, w) for neighbor, w in self.adj_list[v] if neighbor != u]
 else:
 self.adj_list[u].remove(v)
 if not self.directed:
 self.adj_list[v].remove(u)
 
 # Remove from edges list
 self.edges = [(e_u, e_v, w) for e_u, e_v, w in self.edges 
 if not (e_u == u and e_v == v) and not (e_u == v and e_v == u)]
 
 return self
 
 def remove_vertex(self, v):
 """Remove vertex v and all incident edges"""
 if v not in self.vertices:
 return self
 
 # Remove vertex from all adjacency lists
 for vertex in self.adj_list:
 if self.weighted:
 self.adj_list[vertex] = [(neighbor, w) for neighbor, w in self.adj_list[vertex] 
 if neighbor != v]
 else:
 if v in self.adj_list[vertex]:
 self.adj_list[vertex].remove(v)
 
 # Remove vertex from our structures
 del self.adj_list[v]
 self.vertices.remove(v)
 
 # Remove edges involving v
 self.edges = [(e_u, e_v, w) for e_u, e_v, w in self.edges 
 if e_u != v and e_v != v]
 
 return self
 
 def is_adjacent(self, u, v):
 """Check if vertices u and v are adjacent"""
 if u not in self.adj_list:
 return False
 
 if self.weighted:
 return any(neighbor == v for neighbor, w in self.adj_list[u])
 return v in self.adj_list[u]
 
 def get_neighbors(self, v):
 """Return list of all vertices adjacent to v"""
 if v not in self.adj_list:
 return []
 
 if self.weighted:
 return [(neighbor, weight) for neighbor, weight in self.adj_list[v]]
 return self.adj_list[v].copy()
 
 def get_degree(self, v):
 """
 Return degree of vertex v
 For directed graphs: returns (in_degree, out_degree)
 For undirected graphs: returns total degree
 """
 if v not in self.adj_list:
 return 0 if not self.directed else (0, 0)
 
 if self.weighted:
 out_degree = len(self.adj_list[v])
 else:
 out_degree = len(self.adj_list[v])
 
 if self.directed:
 in_degree = 0
 for vertex in self.adj_list:
 if self.weighted:
 in_degree += sum(1 for neighbor, w in self.adj_list[vertex] if neighbor == v)
 else:
 in_degree += 1 if v in self.adj_list[vertex] else 0
 return (in_degree, out_degree)
 
 return out_degree
 
 def is_empty(self):
 """Check if graph has no vertices"""
 return len(self.vertices) == 0
 
 def is_connected(self):
 """Check if the graph is connected (for undirected graphs)"""
 if self.is_empty():
 return True
 
 if self.directed:
 # For directed graphs, check strong connectivity
 return self._is_strongly_connected()
 
 # BFS from first vertex
 start = next(iter(self.vertices))
 visited = set()
 queue = [start]
 visited.add(start)
 
 while queue:
 current = queue.pop(0)
 neighbors = self.get_neighbors(current)
 for neighbor in neighbors:
 if isinstance(neighbor, tuple):
 neighbor = neighbor[0] # Get vertex from (vertex, weight)
 if neighbor not in visited:
 visited.add(neighbor)
 queue.append(neighbor)
 
 return len(visited) == len(self.vertices)
 
 def _is_strongly_connected(self):
 """Check if directed graph is strongly connected"""
 if self.is_empty():
 return True
 
 # Check if path exists from every vertex to every other vertex
 vertices_list = list(self.vertices)
 for start in vertices_list:
 if not self._bfs_reachable(start, vertices_list):
 return False
 return True
 
 def _bfs_reachable(self, start, targets):
 """BFS to check if all targets are reachable from start"""
 visited = set()
 queue = [start]
 visited.add(start)
 
 while queue:
 current = queue.pop(0)
 neighbors = self.get_neighbors(current)
 for neighbor in neighbors:
 if isinstance(neighbor, tuple):
 neighbor = neighbor[0]
 if neighbor not in visited:
 visited.add(neighbor)
 queue.append(neighbor)
 
 return all(t in visited for t in targets)
 
 def display(self):
 """Display the graph"""
 print(f"\nGraph ({'Directed' if self.directed else 'Undirected'}, "
 f"{'Weighted' if self.weighted else 'Unweighted'})")
 print(f"Vertices: {self.vertices}")
 print("Adjacency List:")
 for vertex in self.adj_list:
 print(f" {vertex} -> {self.adj_list[vertex]}")


# Example usage and demonstration
if __name__ == "__main__":
 # Create undirected unweighted graph
 print("=" * 50)
 print("Example 1: Undirected Unweighted Graph")
 print("=" * 50)
 
 g1 = Graph(directed=False, weighted=False)
 g1.add_edge('A', 'B').add_edge('A', 'C').add_edge('B', 'C')
 g1.add_edge('B', 'D').add_edge('C', 'D')
 g1.display()
 
 print(f"\nIs A adjacent to C? {g1.is_adjacent('A', 'C')}")
 print(f"Neighbors of B: {g1.get_neighbors('B')}")
 print(f"Degree of B: {g1.get_degree('B')}")
 print(f"Is connected? {g1.is_connected()}")
 
 # Create directed weighted graph
 print("\n" + "=" * 50)
 print("Example 2: Directed Weighted Graph")
 print("=" * 50)
 
 g2 = Graph(directed=True, weighted=True)
 g2.add_edge('A', 'B', 5).add_edge('A', 'C', 3)
 g2.add_edge('B', 'C', 2).add_edge('B', 'D', 4)
 g2.add_edge('C', 'D', 1)
 g2.display()
 
 print(f"\nNeighbors of B (with weights): {g2.get_neighbors('B')}")
 print(f"In-degree and Out-degree of B: {g2.get_degree('B')}")
 
 # Demonstrate Handshaking Theorem
 print("\n" + "=" * 50)
 print("Handshaking Theorem Verification")
 print("=" * 50)
 
 total_degree = sum(g1.get_degree(v) for v in g1.vertices)
 num_edges = len(g1.edges)
 print(f"Sum of degrees: {total_degree}")
 print(f"2 * |E|: {2 * num_edges}")
 print(f"Verified: {total_degree == 2 * num_edges}")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation uses an adjacency list representation, which is space-efficient for sparse graphs. Key features: (1) Supports both directed and undirected graphs, (2) Supports weighted edges with optional weight parameter, (3) Implements all Graph ADT operations including add/remove vertex/edge, is_adjacent, get_neighbors, get_degree, and is_connected, (4) Uses BFS for connectivity checking, (5) Includes Handshaking Theorem verification in the demo section. Time complexity: O(1) for add_vertex and is_adjacent, O(V) for remove_vertex and get_degree in directed graphs, O(V+E) for is_connected.
examples.timeComplexity: O(V + E) for most operations, O(1) for add_edge

examples.title: C: Graph Implementation with Adjacency Matrix
examples.description: Complete C implementation of Graph ADT using adjacency matrix representation with support for directed, undirected, and weighted graphs
examples.explanation: This C implementation uses an adjacency matrix representation, which provides O(1) edge lookup. Key features: (1) Supports both directed and undirected graphs via the isDirected flag, (2) Supports weighted edges with the isWeighted flag and INF marker for no-edge, (3) Implements all Graph ADT operations including add/remove vertex/edge, is_adjacent, get_neighbors, get_degree, and is_connected, (4) Uses BFS for connectivity checking, (5) Includes vertex labels for user-friendly output, (6) Implements Handshaking Theorem verification for undirected graphs. Time complexity: O(1) for add_edge and is_adjacent (matrix lookup), O(V) for get_neighbors and get_degree, O(V²) for is_connected. Space complexity: O(V²) for the adjacency matrix.
examples.timeComplexity: O(V²) for adjacency matrix, O(V+E) for traversals

examples.title: Java: Graph Implementation with Multiple Representations
examples.description: Complete Java implementation of Graph ADT supporting both adjacency list and matrix representations with full OOP design
examples.explanation: This Java implementation provides a generic, object-oriented Graph ADT with multiple features: (1) Generic type parameter T allows any object as vertex labels, (2) Maintains both adjacency list and adjacency matrix internally for flexibility, (3) Supports directed/undirected and weighted/unweighted graphs, (4) Implements all core Graph ADT operations including add/remove vertex/edge, isAdjacent, getNeighbors, getDegree, and isConnected, (5) Uses BFS for connectivity checking, (6) Includes Handshaking Theorem verification, (7) Provides a fluent interface with method chaining. Time complexity: O(1) for add_edge and isAdjacent (hash lookups), O(V) for getDegree in directed graphs, O(V+E) for isConnected. Space complexity: O(V + E).
examples.timeComplexity: O(V + E) for most operations, O(1) for edge lookup in hash-based representation
